page: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-60 is a general-purpose server instance belonging to the t2d family. It features 60 dedicated physical cores and 60 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 240 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the t2d-standard-60 demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression both rank in the top 10% tier. Small-block cached read-only memory bandwidth also achieves top-tier status, though large-block uncached read and write bandwidths perform in the average range. Single-core performance is moderately strong, with Geekbench single-core scores and single-threaded gzip compression landing in the middle 50% tier.

The instance is highly capable for parallel processing tasks, software compilation, and database operations, as evidenced by strong scores in Passmark database tests and high Redis SET throughput. It also performs well in prompt processing for small and medium language models, though text generation for large 70B models is a notable bottleneck, ranking in the bottom 25%. Given its dedicated physical cores and balanced resource density, the t2d-standard-60 is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads, web serving, and containerized microservices that require consistent CPU allocation without hyperthreading.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-60 is a general-purpose server instance featuring 60 dedicated physical cores and 60 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. Equipped with 240 GB of RAM, it maintains a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU execution and cached memory bandwidth, alongside strong database operations and software compilation capabilities. However, large-block uncached memory transfers and single-threaded operations perform at average levels, and large-scale LLM text generation represents a performance tradeoff. This instance is optimized for multi-threaded workloads, database hosting, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring dedicated CPU resources.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-60 is a general-purpose instance with 60 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 240 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance, ideal for database and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-60 features 60 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 240 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated 60-core AMD EPYC platform delivering balanced memory allocation and top-tier multi-threaded compute performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 60 dedicated physical AMD EPYC 7B13 cores with no hyperthreading.
240 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and cached memory bandwidth.
Strong performance in database operations and software compilation.
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware accelerators.
Suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and databases.
